title_en: Organic vs. Conventional Salad Greens: What's Really On Your Plate?
title_es: Verdes Orgánicos vs. Convencionales: ¿Qué Hay Realmente en Tu Plato?
hook_en: Look at this bag — triple-washed, but still full of pesticides!
hook_es: ¡Mira esta bolsa — lavada tres veces, pero aún llena de pesticidas!
key_message: Even after triple-washing, conventional greens can have multiple pesticide residues; organic is cleaner and richer in nutrients.
visual_elements: Shot of a conventional salad greens bag with 'triple wash' label
Close-up of conventional greens in water showing residue
Comparison shot of fresh organic greens being rinsed
Text overlay: '7 pesticide residues vs. organic purity!'
_source_food_context:
DAILY STAPLE COMPARISON [Produce]:
• HEALTHY: Organic salad greens (lettuce, spinach, kale, arugula — grown without synthetic chemicals)
• PROCESSED/CONVENTIONAL: Conventional salad greens (sprayed with pesticides, often pre-washed with chlorine solutions)
• KEY FACT: Leafy greens are on the Dirty Dozen list because their large surface area absorbs more pesticide residue. Spinach samples had an average of 7 different pesticide residues. Organic greens also tend to have higher polyphenol content
• VISUAL IDEA: Show the triple-wash label on a conventional bag — 'washed 3 times and there's still pesticide residue. Imagine what's on the unwashed ones'

title_es: Duelo de Espinacas: ¡Orgánica vs. Llena de Pesticidas!
hook_en: Bet you didn't know your spinach had a dirty secret!
hook_es: ¡Apuesto a que no sabías que tu espinaca tiene un secreto sucio!
key_message: Conventional spinach is one of the dirtiest produce items; organic spinach offers a healthier, safer alternative.
visual_elements: Close-up of a conventional spinach bag with a pesticide warning
Side-by-side of a dirty dozen list highlighting spinach
Pouring out organic spinach onto a clean, white towel
